# Solution

## (a) Implementation of `Canonicalize`

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, max(x.width, PAD))
}
```

This implementation:
- Preserves the kind and numeric value (never changing which entity the id refers to)
- Sets the width to the maximum of the current width and the minimum PAD (4 digits)
- Is a no-op when the id is already canonical (width ≥ 4)

## (b) Specification Lemma

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); Wellformed(c)
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); c.kind == x.kind
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); c.value == x.value
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); c.width >= PAD
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); c.width >= x.width
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); x.width >= PAD ==> c.width == x.width
{ }
```

**Explanation of ensures clauses:**

1. **`Wellformed(c)`**: The canonicalized id is wellformed (width can hold the value)
2. **`c.kind == x.kind`**: The kind is never changed
3. **`c.value == x.value`**: The numeric value is never changed
4. **`c.width >= PAD`**: The result is always at least 4 digits wide
5. **`c.width >= x.width`**: The width never decreases
6. **`x.width >= PAD ==> c.width == x.width`**: Canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op

Together, these clauses capture that canonicalization is a monotonic width-adjustment operation that preserves identity while enforcing a minimum display width of 4 digits.
